chapter goal: How do incidence theorems become bracket expressions that can be checked and transformed?
computational checks: cross-ratio invariance under a projective reparametrization
join/meet incidence for generated homogeneous points
mode-specific conic or hyperbolic numeric check when relevant
gaps: Proof-heavy material in Chapter 15 is represented by bracket diagrams, determinant/cross-ratio checks, or small parameter experiments rather than copied text.

visual sequence.expected_observation: Rescaling homogeneous data changes determinants individually, not the invariant relation.
visual sequence.learner_inspection_target: For Chapter 15, inspect desargues configuration by identifying which objects instantiate desargues theorem.
visual sequence.library: numpy, matplotlib, local projective/conic/Cayley-Klein helpers, and self-contained HTML
visual sequence.validation_target: determinant signs and Grassmann-Plucker-style identities are stable
